name: fflags
description: Accumulates floating-point exception flags. Each bit is sticky: set on a matching exception and cleared only by explicit software write.

fields.name: NV
fields.description: Invalid Operation: set when a floating-point operation produces an invalid result (e.g., 0/0, ∞−∞, √−1).

fields.name: DZ
fields.description: Divide by Zero: set when a finite non-zero value is divided by zero.

fields.name: OF
fields.description: Overflow: set when the rounded result exceeds the representable range.

fields.name: UF
fields.description: Underflow: set when the result is tiny and suffers a loss of precision.

fields.name: NX
fields.description: Inexact: set when the rounded result differs from the mathematical result.

name: frm
description: Specifies the dynamic rounding mode used by floating-point instructions that use DYN rounding. Also aliased in fcsr[7:5].

values.label: RNE — Round to Nearest, ties to Even
RMM — Round to Nearest, ties to Max Magnitude
DYN — Dynamic (use frm field; only valid in instruction encoding)

name: fcsr
description: Combines the floating-point rounding mode (frm) and accrued exception flags (fflags) into a single CSR. Bits 31:8 are reserved for future standard extensions.

values.label: RNE — Round to Nearest, ties to Even
RMM — Round to Nearest, ties to Max Magnitude
DYN — Dynamic (use frm field; only valid in instruction encoding)

fields.name: DZ
fields.description: Divide by Zero accrued exception flag.

name: seed
description: Provides access to a hardware entropy source (Zkr). Read with csrrw to poll for fresh entropy; the write value is ignored. Access requires mseccfg.SSEED/USEED to be set.

fields.name: OPST
fields.description: Operation status: indicates whether entropy is available.

values.label: ES16 — 16 bits of entropy available in bits[15:0]

fields.name: entropy
fields.description: 16 bits of randomness, valid only when OPST=ES16. Reads as 0 in other states.

name: vstart
description: Specifies the index of the first element to be executed by a vector instruction. Normally written to 0 by hardware after each vector instruction. Upper bits are WARL (implementation-defined width).

fields.name: vstart
fields.description: Start element index. Number of writable bits is implementation-defined (at most log2(VLEN)).

name: vxsat
description: Single-bit sticky flag set when a vector fixed-point instruction saturates. Also mirrored in vcsr[0]. Write 0 to clear.

fields.name: vxsat
fields.description: Saturation flag: set when a fixed-point instruction saturates the result.

name: vxrm
description: Selects the rounding mode for vector fixed-point arithmetic instructions. Also mirrored in vcsr[2:1].

values.label: rod — Round to Odd (OR bits into LSB)

name: vcsr
description: Provides unified access to vxrm and vxsat. Writes to vcsr are reflected in vxrm and vxsat and vice versa.

values.label: rod — Round to Odd (OR bits into LSB)

name: vl
description: Read-only register holding the number of elements to be updated by a vector instruction. Set by vsetvl/vsetvli/vsetivli.

fields.name: vl
fields.description: Active vector length in elements. Range: 0 to VLMAX.

name: vtype
description: Read-only register set by vsetvl/vsetvli/vsetivli. Encodes element width, register grouping, and agnostic policies. vill=1 means an unsupported configuration was requested.

fields.name: vill
fields.description: Illegal configuration: set when vsetvl writes an unsupported vtype. All other bits read as 0 when vill=1.

fields.name: vma
fields.description: Vector Mask Agnostic: when set, masked-off elements may be written with all 1s or left undisturbed.

fields.name: vta
fields.description: Vector Tail Agnostic: when set, tail elements (beyond vl) may be written with all 1s or left undisturbed.

fields.name: vsew
fields.description: Selected Element Width: encodes the element data type width.

fields.name: vlmul
fields.description: Vector Length Multiplier: controls register grouping (LMUL). Signed 3-bit value; negative values represent fractional LMUL.

name: vlenb
description: Read-only constant holding VLEN/8 — the number of bytes in a single vector register. Useful for computing stride values without disturbing vl/vtype.

fields.name: vlenb
fields.description: Bytes per vector register = VLEN/8. Design-time constant.

name: sstatus
description: Tracks and controls the hart's current operating state; a restricted view of mstatus.

fields.name: SD
fields.description: Dirty summary: set if FS, VS, or XS == Dirty.

fields.name: UXL
fields.description: User XLEN: controls XLEN for U-mode (RV64 only).

fields.name: SDT
fields.description: S-mode disable-trap bit (Ssdbltrp): set on trap entry, cleared by SRET. Prevents double-trap from being delivered to S-mode.

fields.name: SPELP
fields.description: Supervisor previous expected-landing-pad state (Zicfilp): holds ELP prior to trap into S-mode.

fields.name: MXR
fields.description: Make eXecutable Readable: when set, loads from execute-only pages succeed.

fields.name: SUM
fields.description: Supervisor User Memory access: when set, S-mode may access U-mode pages.

fields.name: XS
fields.description: Extension state summary for user-mode extensions.

values.label: None dirty or clean, some on
Off — unit disabled or context not initialized
Initial — context is at initial (clean) value
Clean — context may differ from initial but matches last save
Dirty — context has been modified since last save
Off — unit disabled or context not initialized
Initial — context is at initial (clean) value
Clean — context may differ from initial but matches last save
Dirty — context has been modified since last save

fields.name: SPP
fields.description: Supervisor Previous Privilege: privilege mode before trap into S-mode.

fields.name: UBE
fields.description: U-mode endianness: 0=little-endian, 1=big-endian for U-mode explicit accesses.

fields.name: SPIE
fields.description: Supervisor Previous Interrupt Enable: value of SIE prior to trap.

fields.name: SIE
fields.description: Supervisor Interrupt Enable: when set, interrupts are enabled in S-mode.

name: sie
description: Controls which interrupts are enabled in supervisor mode. Writable subset of mie.

name: stvec
description: Holds the trap-vector base address and mode for supervisor traps.

fields.name: BASE
fields.description: Trap-handler base address (4-byte aligned; lower 2 bits always zero when used as address).

values.label: Direct — all traps set pc to BASE
Vectored — exceptions to BASE, interrupts to BASE+4×cause

name: scounteren
description: Controls availability of performance counters to U-mode.

name: senvcfg
description: Controls certain characteristics of the U-mode execution environment.

fields.name: PMM
fields.description: Pointer masking mode for U-mode (Ssnpm). Read-only zero when not implemented or RV32.

fields.name: CBZE
fields.description: Cache Block Zero (CBO.ZERO) enable for U-mode (Zicboz). 0=illegal, 1=enabled.

fields.name: CBCFE
fields.description: Cache Block Clean/Flush (CBO.CLEAN/FLUSH) enable for U-mode (Zicbom).

fields.name: CBIE
fields.description: Cache Block Invalidate (CBO.INVAL) enable and behavior for U-mode (Zicbom).

fields.name: FIOM
fields.description: Fence of I/O implies Memory: in U-mode, FENCE with I/O bits also orders memory accesses.

name: sscratch
description: Dedicated scratch register for supervisor mode; typically holds a pointer to the hart-local supervisor context while running user code.

name: sepc
description: Holds the virtual address of the instruction that was interrupted or caused an exception when a trap is taken into S-mode.

fields.name: PC
fields.description: Exception program counter (bits XLEN-1:1). Bit 0 is always 0.

name: scause
description: Written by hardware when a trap is taken into S-mode; encodes the trap cause.

fields.name: Interrupt
fields.description: 1 = trap caused by an interrupt; 0 = synchronous exception.

fields.name: Exception Code
fields.description: Identifies the last exception (Interrupt=0) or interrupt (Interrupt=1).

name: stval
description: Written with exception-specific information when a trap is taken into S-mode (e.g., faulting address or illegal instruction bits).

fields.name: STVAL
fields.description: Trap value (faulting address, or instruction bits for illegal-instruction traps, or 0).

name: sip
description: Shows pending interrupts visible to supervisor mode; writable subset of mip.

fields.name: SEIP
fields.description: Supervisor external interrupt pending. Read-only (set/cleared by execution environment).

fields.name: STIP
fields.description: Supervisor timer interrupt pending. Read-only (reflects stimecmp comparison when Sstc is implemented).

fields.name: SSIP
fields.description: Supervisor software interrupt pending. Writable by S-mode software.

name: stimecmp
description: A timer interrupt becomes pending (STIP) when time >= stimecmp (Sstc extension).

fields.name: STIMECMP
fields.description: 64-bit unsigned comparison value. STIP is set when time >= stimecmp.

name: satp
long_name: Supervisor Address Translation and Protection Register
description: Controls supervisor-mode address translation: holds root page table PPN, ASID, and translation mode.

fields.name: MODE (RV64)
fields.description: Address-translation mode (RV64). Writing an unsupported value has no effect.

fields.name: ASID (RV64)
fields.description: Address Space ID (RV64, up to 16 bits). Facilitates per-address-space TLB flushing.

fields.name: PPN (RV64)
fields.description: Physical Page Number of root page table (RV64, 44 bits = PA >> 12).

name: mstatus
description: Tracks and controls the hart's current operating state. A restricted view appears as sstatus.

fields.name: SD
fields.description: Dirty summary: set (RO) when FS, VS, or XS == Dirty.

fields.name: MBE
fields.description: M-mode endianness: 0=little-endian, 1=big-endian for M-mode explicit memory accesses.

fields.name: SBE
fields.description: S-mode endianness: 0=little-endian, 1=big-endian for S-mode explicit memory accesses.

fields.name: SXL
fields.description: S-mode XLEN (RV64): controls XLEN for S-mode.

fields.name: UXL
fields.description: U-mode XLEN (RV64): controls XLEN for U-mode.

fields.name: SDT
fields.description: S-mode disable-trap (Ssdbltrp): mirrors sstatus.SDT; controls double-trap handling in S-mode.

fields.name: SPELP
fields.description: Supervisor previous expected-landing-pad (Zicfilp): holds ELP prior to S-mode trap.

fields.name: TSR
fields.description: Trap SRET: when set, SRET executed in S-mode raises an illegal-instruction exception.

fields.name: TW
fields.description: Timeout Wait: when set, WFI in less-privileged modes may raise illegal-instruction exception.

fields.name: TVM
fields.description: Trap Virtual Memory: when set, satp access and SFENCE.VMA in S-mode trap to M-mode.

fields.name: MXR
fields.description: Make eXecutable Readable: when set, loads from execute-only pages succeed.

fields.name: SUM
fields.description: Supervisor User Memory access: when set, S-mode may access U-mode pages.

fields.name: MPRV
fields.description: Modify PRiVilege: when set, M-mode memory accesses use the privilege level in MPP.

fields.name: XS
fields.description: Extension state summary for user-mode extensions.

values.label: None dirty or clean, some on

Clean — context may differ from initial but matches last save
Dirty — context has been modified since last save

name: misa
description: Reports the ISA supported by the hart. Returns 0 if not implemented.

fields.name: MXL
fields.description: Machine XLEN (read-only): native base integer ISA width.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Extension 'N': Tentatively reserved for User-Level Interrupts.

fields.name: E
fields.description: Extension 'E': RV32E/64E base ISA (read-only complement of I).

name: medeleg
description: Bit i set = exception i is delegated to S-mode trap handler (when occurring in S or U mode).

fields.name: DblTrap
fields.description: Double trap (code 16) delegation — always read-only zero.

fields.name: MEcall
fields.description: M-mode environment call (code 11) — always read-only zero (never delegatable).

name: mideleg
description: Bit i set = interrupt i is delegated to S-mode. Bit layout matches mip/mie.

fields.name: MEID
fields.description: Machine external interrupt delegation — normally read-only zero.

fields.name: MTID
fields.description: Machine timer interrupt delegation — normally read-only zero.

fields.name: MSID
fields.description: Machine software interrupt delegation — normally read-only zero.

name: mie
description: Controls which interrupts are enabled in machine mode.

name: mtvec
description: Holds the machine-mode trap-vector base address and mode.

fields.name: BASE
fields.description: Trap-handler base address (4-byte aligned; lower 2 bits are always zero when used as address).

values.label: Direct — all traps set pc to BASE
Vectored — exceptions to BASE, interrupts to BASE+4×cause

fields.name: PBMTE

fields.name: ADUE

name: mscratch
description: Dedicated scratch register for M-mode; typically holds a pointer to the hart-local M-mode context.

name: mepc
description: Holds the virtual address of the instruction that was interrupted or caused an exception when a trap is taken into M-mode.

fields.name: PC
fields.description: Exception program counter (bits XLEN-1:1). Bit 0 is always 0.

name: mcause
description: Written by hardware when a trap is taken into M-mode; encodes the trap cause.

fields.name: Interrupt
fields.description: 1 = trap caused by an interrupt; 0 = synchronous exception.

fields.name: Exception Code
fields.description: Identifies the last exception or interrupt cause.

name: mtval
description: Written with exception-specific information when a trap is taken into M-mode (faulting address, illegal instruction bits, or 0).

fields.name: MTVAL
fields.description: Trap value (faulting virtual address, instruction bits for illegal-instruction traps, or 0).

name: mip
description: Shows which interrupts are currently pending. Some bits are read-only (set by hardware).

fields.name: MEIP
fields.description: Machine external interrupt pending. Read-only (set by platform interrupt controller).

fields.name: SEIP
fields.description: Supervisor external interrupt pending. Writable by M-mode; OR'd with external interrupt signal.

fields.name: MTIP
fields.description: Machine timer interrupt pending. Read-only (cleared by writing memory-mapped mtimecmp).

fields.name: STIP
fields.description: Supervisor timer interrupt pending. Writable by M-mode (or RO when Sstc implemented).

fields.name: MSIP
fields.description: Machine software interrupt pending. Read-only (written via memory-mapped msip register).

fields.name: SSIP
fields.description: Supervisor software interrupt pending. Writable by M-mode.

name: mtinst
description: Written with a pseudo-instruction encoding when a trap is taken into M-mode from a virtual machine (hypervisor extension).

name: mtval2
description: Used by the hypervisor extension to provide additional trap information (e.g., guest physical address for guest-page faults).

fields.name: MTVAL2
fields.description: Secondary trap value (e.g., guest PA on guest page fault, or 0).

name: mvendorid
description: Read-only register providing the JEDEC manufacturer ID of the core provider. Returns 0 if not implemented.

fields.name: Bank
fields.description: Number of JEDEC continuation codes (bank number minus one).

fields.name: Offset
fields.description: Final byte of JEDEC manufacturer ID (parity bit discarded).

name: marchid
description: Read-only register encoding the base microarchitecture. Open-source IDs have MSB=0; commercial IDs have MSB=1.

fields.name: Architecture ID
fields.description: Microarchitecture identifier. MSB=0 for open-source, MSB=1 for commercial.

name: mimpid
description: Read-only register encoding the version of the processor implementation.

fields.name: Implementation
fields.description: Implementation version (format defined by the implementor).

name: mhartid
description: Read-only register containing the integer ID of the hardware thread. One hart must have ID 0.

fields.name: Hart ID
fields.description: Unique hardware thread identifier within the execution environment.

name: mconfigptr
description: Read-only register holding the physical address of the configuration data structure, or 0 if not implemented.

fields.name: Pointer
fields.description: Physical address of the configuration data structure (must be 8-byte aligned), or 0.

name: mcycle
description: 64-bit counter tracking the number of clock cycles executed on the processor core. Shared between harts on the same core.

fields.name: Count
fields.description: Cycle count (64-bit on RV32 and RV64; mcycleh holds bits 63:32 on RV32).

name: minstret
description: 64-bit counter tracking the number of instructions the hart has retired.

fields.name: Count
fields.description: Instructions-retired count (64-bit on RV32 and RV64; minstreth holds bits 63:32 on RV32).

name: mstatush
description: Upper 32 bits of mstatus for RV32. Holds endianness control bits MBE and SBE.

name: mseccfg
description: Controls PMP security policies (Smepmp), seed CSR access (Zkr), and other security-related settings.

fields.name: MLPE
fields.description: Machine Landing Pad Enable (Zicfilp): enables landing-pad enforcement for M-mode indirect calls/jumps.

fields.name: PMM
fields.description: Pointer Masking Mode for M-mode (Smmpm): controls pointer masking tag width.

fields.name: USEED
fields.description: U-mode seed access (Zkr): when set, U-mode may read the seed CSR.

fields.name: SSEED
fields.description: S-mode seed access (Zkr): when set, S-mode may read the seed CSR.

fields.name: MML
fields.description: Machine Mode Lockdown (Smepmp): tightens PMP permissions; M-mode must match a PMP entry to execute.

fields.name: MMWP
fields.description: Machine Mode Whitelist Policy (Smepmp): when set, M-mode accesses denied unless matched by a PMP entry.

fields.name: RLB
fields.description: Rule Locking Bypass (Smepmp): when set, allows modification of locked PMP entries (must be cleared before locking).

name: mhpmevent3
description: Selects the event counted by mhpmcounter3. Event encoding is implementation-defined. Sscofpmf adds mode-inhibit and overflow bits in the upper portion.

fields.name: MINH
fields.description: Machine-mode count inhibit (Sscofpmf): when set, counter does not increment in M-mode.

fields.name: OF
fields.description: Overflow flag (Sscofpmf): set when counter overflows from all-ones to zero. Sticky; write 0 to clear.

fields.name: EVENT
fields.description: Implementation-defined event selector. Selects which hardware event is counted.
